title: Nginx proxy — forward /api requests from frontend to backend
description: Configure the nginx server in the web container to proxy /api/* requests to the FastAPI backend, so the frontend doesn't need to hardcode the API URL.
acceptanceCriteria: Requests to /api/* from the browser are proxied to http://api:8000/api/*
Frontend Axios base URL can be set to '' (empty) or '/' (relative) instead of http://localhost:8000
Static frontend assets are served correctly
docker-compose up brings up the full stack and the app works end-to-end

steps.description: Update nginx.conf to add API proxy
steps.details: In src/web/nginx.conf, add a location block: location /api/ { proxy_pass http://api:8000/api/; proxy_set_header Host $host; proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for; }. The existing location / block should still serve the React app's index.html for client-side routing (try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html).

steps.description: Update Axios base URL to use relative path
steps.details: In src/web/src/lib/api.ts, update the Axios instance baseURL from 'http://localhost:8000' to '' (empty string). This makes all requests go to the same origin, and nginx handles the /api/ proxying. Also update CORS in FastAPI to allow the nginx origin.

Run docker-compose up --build in src/. Access http://localhost:3001. Verify: sign up works, login works, weight entries can be logged. Check browser network tab to confirm /api/* requests go to localhost:3001 (same origin) and return correct responses.

technicalNotes: In docker-compose, services communicate by service name — 'api' resolves to the API container
Without try_files, direct navigation to /dashboard would return 404 from nginx
